name: background-tasks
description: Pi-native runtime tools for long-running and state-waiting work — background a command and get woken on exit (or first pattern match), poll a command until a condition holds (CI green, file appears, log line) or it times out, and list/tail/status/cancel the job registry.

interface.shortDescription: Background long commands, monitor conditions, manage jobs
interface.longDescription: A pi-native runtime extension that gives the agent three tools for work that should not block a turn: background runs a command detached and wakes the agent on exit (or the first time output matches a pattern); monitor polls a command on an interval until a condition holds (exit_zero, exit_nonzero, stdout_matches, stdout_not_matches) or a timeout fires; jobs lists, tails, gets the status of, or cancels any background job or monitor. The tools are pi-runtime-only; the bundled skill is the portable description of when and how to reach for them.
defaultPrompt: Use background-tasks to run a long command in the background, poll a command until a condition holds, or manage the job registry.
